provenance_line: Fitzpatrick Lab, Dartmouth College | Numerical N=4 TLS: amplitude and multi-pulse (two-pulse) control

colour_legend.green: Physical metadata / simulation INPUT feature (set in the script)

rows.value_or_range: Amplitude and multi-pulse control of a 4-TLS ensemble
rows.derivation_or_notes: a: ring-down map (single pulse); b: amplitude sweep at the optimal drive frequency (collapse-and-revival); c: two-pulse protocol (A2 swept, A1 fixed, gap 100 ns).

sections.section: PHYSICAL SYSTEM METADATA - the simulated TLS ensemble

rows.value_or_range: N = 4 coupled TLS, Lindblad master equation

rows.derivation_or_notes: Bare frequencies drawn with a fixed seed; the resulting w_i/2pi = 3.49, 3.17, 4.10, 4.19 GHz.

rows.value_or_range: J/2pi in [-50, 50] MHz (code uniform(-0.05, 0.05)), XX only
rows.derivation_or_notes: Random symmetric dipole-dipole couplings between every pair.

rows.value_or_range: 3.0-5.0 GHz, 400 pts (panel a); optimal wd/2pi = 4.15 GHz (panels b,c)
rows.derivation_or_notes: Swept in the ring-down map; fixed at the ring-down-maximizing freq for b,c.

rows.value_or_range: A1/2pi = 100 MHz base (code 0.10); sweeps 0 -> 0.10 in 60 steps
rows.derivation_or_notes: Fixed for the ring-down map; swept 0->0.10 for the amplitude panels (pulse-1 in b, pulse-2 in c).

rows.value_or_range: single pulse: 200 ns; two-pulse: 200 ns / gap 100 ns / 200 ns
rows.derivation_or_notes: tau = 200 ns; two-pulse protocol with inter-pulse gap tau_g = 100 ns.
Collective excitation; ring-down, amplitude sweep, two-pulse sweep.

actual_stored_columns.note: The schema above (in_a_stored_data_file = No) describes the original plotting workflow, which stored nothing. THIS dataset persists the features as a long-format table; below is how each pkl/csv row is laid out.

column_notes.panel_id: 0 ring-down map, 1 amplitude sweep, 2 two-pulse sweep
column_notes.amp1: pulse-1 amplitude; swept in panel 1; fixed 0.10 else (green)
column_notes.amp2: pulse-2 amplitude; swept in panel 2 (A1 fixed); 0 else (green)
